channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Prosecutor says Salman Rushdie was too stunned to react when a man started to stab him
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Salman Rushdie was so stunned when a masked man started to stab him on a stage in western New York that the acclaimed author didn't even try to fight back, a prosecutor said Monday during opening statements in the suspect's attempted murder trial.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday. Here’s what you need to know. Starbucks The coffee chain is offering its rewards members a free tall hot or iced brewed coffee any time during “Starbucks Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Vatican charity says Trump administration's gutting of USAID is 'reckless' and could kill millions
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The Vatican's charity said Monday that U.S. plans to gut USAID were “reckless” and could kill millions, while Pope Francis’ point man on development urged the Trump administration to remember Christian principles about caring for others.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Nearly two dozen states sue the Trump Administration to halt cuts in medical research funding
item.description: Attorney generals from 22 states have filed a lawsuit against the Trump Administration for slashing funding for medical and public health research at universities nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's latest tariff plans on steel, aluminum and other imports are spreading uncertainty
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump plans to on Monday formally announce 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports as part of an aggressive effort to remake the existing terms of world trade that so far has compounded economic uncertainty. Trump also intends this week to reset U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fired head of the agency that protects whistleblowers sues Trump, saying he was ousted illegally
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The recently fired head of the Office of Special Counsel, a federal agency dedicated to protecting whistleblowers, sued President Donald Trump on Monday, saying he was illegally ousted as part of the president’s massive overhaul of the government.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Space telescope spots rare 'Einstein ring' of light around galaxy in our cosmic neighborhood
item.description: Europe’s Euclid space telescope has detected a rare halo of bright light around a nearby galaxy, astronomers reported Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Crowd gathers to visit Bob's Red Mill Restaurant final time
item.description: A crowd gathered outside the restaurant on Sunday, the final day of operation, to visit one last time.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Foran, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer warns of 'Trump shutdown,' lays out 4-pronged plan for Democrats
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) in a letter to colleagues Monday warned of the possibility of a “Trump shutdown” and reminded fellow senators that Democrats have the power to make or break any bill to fund the government past March 14.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Rudd, Edwards named newest NASCAR Hall of Fame inductees
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — One of the toughest drivers in NASCAR history joined one the most enigmatic drivers of the modern era as the newest inductees into the Hall of Fame in a star-studded Friday night ceremony.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel nominated as Ohio's lieutenant governor
item.description: Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine is nominating former Ohio State Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel as the state's next lieutenant governor.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Third judge blocks Trump’s order ending birthright citizenship for kids of people in US illegally
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A third federal judge on Monday blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S. illegally. The ruling from U.S. District Judge Joseph N.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl halftime performer detained after unfurling Sudanese-Palestinian flag
item.description: A performer in Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime show was detained late Sunday after showing a Sudanese-Palestinian flag on the field.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA's rich and famous made 'odd request' of private armies as wildfires fueled fear, boss says
item.description: A private security expert details what it has been like protecting residents in wealthy communities ravaged by the wildfires and why the need has increased in the aftermath.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taylor Swift booed by heavily pro-Eagles crowd at the Super Bowl
item.description: Taylor Swift was back at the Super Bowl to watch Travis Kelce and his Kansas City Chiefs play the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday. Her reception was a whole lot different than last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Eagles beat Chiefs 40-22 in lopsided Super Bowl LIX
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs wanted to make history with three back-to-back-to-back championships, but Super Bowl LIX was dominated by the Philadelphia Eagles, who won the game 40-22.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Marine from California identified as one of 4 people killed in Philippines plane crash
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (AP) — A U.S. Marine from California was identified by military officials Sunday as one of four people killed when a plane contracted by the U.S. military crashed in a rice field in the southern Philippines. Sgt. Jacob M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stage and film actor Tony Roberts dies at 85
item.description: Tony Roberts, a versatile, Tony Award-nominated theater performer at home in both plays and musicals and who appeared in several Woody Allen movies — often as Allen's best friend — has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Elon Musk is calling for the impeachment of the federal judge who made a decision early Saturday morning that the Treasury Department should block access to anyone “other than civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties” from its payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Drivers targeted in emerging crime trend have one thing in common
item.description: Texas law enforcement issued a word of warning to Ford truck owners after they arrested a trio of thieves who targeted the high-end truck model by stealing taillights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What makes a dog a winner? A Westminster Kennel Club judge explains
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The competitors can come in forms as different as a Chihuahua, a great Dane, a bulldog and a greyhound. Each one is a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canada beats US 3-1 in Game 5 to win the Rivalry Series women's hockey showcase
item.description: Jennifer Gardiner broke a tie with 6:44 left and Canada beat the United States 3-1 on Saturday night to win the Rivalry Series women's hockey showcase for the third straight time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: Hawaii’s sunshine, short rain showers and clean air together create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mcavoy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, WGN 9

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KRON4

item.title: 2 people charged in fatal stabbing of Fort Campbell soldier in Tennessee, police say
item.description: CLARKSVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Two people have been charged in the death of a Fort Campbell soldier who was stabbed nearly 70 times last year, police said Saturday. Sofia Rodas, 35, has been charged with first-degree murder and tampering with evidence in the death of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family files lawsuit after mother, son die from food poisoning while on vacation
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, WGN 9

item.title: A 15-year-old went to a Brooklyn parade. The NYPD wrongly accused him of a mass shooting
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Camden Lee was leaving high school football practice in September when he saw the photograph, splashed across the New York Police Department’s social media accounts, that would soon upend his life.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: T-Swift fan, Philly agency trolling each other ahead of Super Bowl LIX
item.description: A Taylor Swift fan from Boston and a Philadelphia-based advertising agency have spent the week leading up to Super Bowl LIX trolling each other with billboards in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, Fox 5

item.title: Blake Lively is sued by Texas crisis specialist in latest 'It Ends With Us' lawsuit
item.description: A Texas crisis communications specialist has sued Blake Lively for defamation after the actor pulled him into her legal fight with co-star and director Justin Baldoni over their film, “It Ends With Us.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, WGN 9
